title: Department of Industrial Relations: apprenticeship pilot program.
subject: DepartmentofIndustrialRelations
apprenticeshippilotprogram

abstracts.abstract:
Existing law provides for the establishment of apprenticeship programs in various trades, to be approved by the Chief of the Division of Apprenticeship Standards within the Department of Industrial Relations in any trade in the state or in a city or trade area whenever the apprentice training needs justify the establishment. 

This bill would require, on or before January 1, 2028, the Department of Industrial Relations (DIR) and the Department of Human Resources to partner with the bargaining units representing employees of DIR to design and develop an apprenticeship pilot program that addresses DIR's staffing challenges. The bill would require the design, development, and administration of the program to meet specified requirements, including being consistent with the constitutional merit principle applicable to civil service employment. The bill would require the program to meet specified requirements, including that the apprenticeship program participants' pay be determined by the collective bargaining process. The bill would include related legislative findings and declarations.

actions.description: Introduced. Read first time. To Com. on RLS. for assignment. To print.
From printer. May be acted upon on or after March 22.
Referred to Com. on L., P.E. & R.
From committee with author's amendments. Read second time and amended. Re-referred to Com. on L., P.E. & R.

extras.impact_clause: An act to add Article 9 (commencing with Section 3150) to Chapter 4 of Division 3 of the Labor Code, relating to employment.
